result.summary: Uber exposes multiple developer-facing HTTP APIs (Riders, Eats Marketplace, Direct, Guest Rides, Vouchers, Driver, Business Receipts, Suppliers/Vehicles, Ads, and Pay) plus a cataloged public MCP endpoint at mcp.uber.com.
result.description: Uber is a transportation, delivery, and business platform that offers ride-hailing, food delivery, courier delivery, payments, advertising, and enterprise workflow products. Its developer program provides APIs for integrating these services into partner applications and business systems.

uber_oauth_app.label: Uber developer application (client ID and client secret)
uber_oauth_app.setup: Sign in to the [Uber Developer Dashboard](https://developer.uber.com/dashboard), create an application from [Create App](https://developer.uber.com/dashboard/create), then open the app's Auth/Application settings to copy its `client_id` and `client_secret`. Add your `redirect_uri` there if you will use authorization-code flows. Some product APIs require Uber approval or enterprise access before the app can use the needed scopes.

uber_server_token.label: Uber Riders server token (legacy)
uber_server_token.setup: Open your existing Riders app in the [Uber Developer Dashboard](https://developer.uber.com/dashboard), go to the Auth tab, and copy the app's `server token`. Uber documents that new server tokens are no longer granted; only existing legacy tokens continue to work until migration off server tokens is complete.

uber_personal_access_token.label: Uber personal user access token for testing
uber_personal_access_token.setup: For quick testing, open your app in the [Uber Developer Dashboard](https://developer.uber.com/dashboard), go to the Auth tab, select scopes, and in “Test with a Personal Access Token” click "Generate a new token". The token is shown once and represents the Uber account currently signed into the dashboard.
